title: od-lean: machine-checked Lean 4 formalisation of the GBFS OD identifiability bounds
description: Lean 4 / Mathlib formal-proof companion to the manuscript 'Standard compliance bounds origin–destination identifiability in GBFS bike-sharing feeds' (R. Fossé and G. Pallares, in preparation for Computer Standards & Interfaces). The deductive content of all three bounds is machine-checked with zero 'sorry', depending only on the three standard Lean axioms (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound): (i) Bound 1, the q⁻¹ Cramér–Rao information limit, with the Cramér–Rao inequality itself discharged from Cauchy–Schwarz on Mathlib's covariance/variance, the score = −ε⁻¹·Π⊥φ / Fisher = ε⁻²·Var(Π⊥φ) derivation, and a Huber–White sandwich proving the q⁻¹ law survives model misspecification; (ii) Bound 2, the bias structure as the algebra of the two-way-centring orthogonal projection off the additive station-effect subspace (gauge freedom, bias = non-separable log-selection, the censoring-vs-aliasing dichotomy); (iii) Bound 3, the collection-horizon law (bias–variance balance giving the δ⁻⁴/δ⁻² exponents, the minimal horizon T* and its q⁻¹ scaling, the regime crossover). The genuinely analytic inputs — the entropic-OT sample-complexity rate (Genevay; Mena–Niles-Weed), the Cramér–Rao regularity conditions, and the Gibbs/Sinkhorn structural model — are isolated as explicit hypotheses. A SymPy script cross-checks the closed forms independently of Lean.
notes: Formal-proof companion to the Computer Standards & Interfaces submission. Toolchain: Lean v4.31.0, Mathlib pinned in lake-manifest.json. Build: lake exe cache get && lake build. The .lake build directory is not included; oleans regenerate from the pinned Mathlib.
